schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill
schema.basis: folder-local candidate promotion with safety gate

theory.canonical_statement: Classical communication is bounded by the speed of light: no signal travels faster than $c$. Quantum entanglement appears to transcend this — entangled particles exhibit correlations across arbitrary distances instantaneously — but the no communication theorem forbids using entanglement alone to transmit information faster than light. The Azimuthal Quantum Communication system, developed across the corpus, works within these constraints while exploiting the topological structure of the TZP framework to achieve communication architectures impossible in ordinary quantum information theory. It uses the teleportation manifold, the atemporal channel structure, and the unified entanglement meta theorem to build a communication system organised around the DAANSphere's 153,600 node addressing — the "Azimuthal" system that gives the volume its name.
theory.technical_interpretation: network. --- ## Part Four: The Unified Entanglement Meta-Theorem ### The Master Result The corpus develops a unified entanglement meta-theorem (ID 0522) that ties the communication system together. The meta-theorem states, in essence, that **all the entanglement resources in the Azimuthal system are unified by a single conserved quantity — the total topological charge** (ID 025). The meta-theorem has the structure: for any configuration of entangled resources on the DAANSphere, the sum of the entanglement entropies, weighted by the winding numbers, equals the total topological charge: This means entanglement cannot be created or destroyed arbitrarily — it can only be moved, swapped, and reconfigured, subject to the conservation of total topological charge. **Entanglement, in the Azimuthal system, is a conserved topological quantity** — the communication-theoretic expression of the conser In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

dictionary_sections.definition: Azimuthal Quantum Communication is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation \sum_i w_i\, S_\text{ent}(i) = Q_\text{total} = \text{conserved}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: Classical communication is bounded by the speed of light: no signal travels faster than $c$. Quantum entanglement appears to transcend this — entangled particles exhibit correlations across arbitrary distances instantaneously — but the no communication theorem forbids using entanglement alone to transmit information faster than light. The Azimuthal Quantum Communication system, developed across the corpus, works within these constraints while exploiting the topological structure of the TZP framework to achieve communication architectures impossible in ordinary quantum information theory. It uses the teleportation manifold, the atemporal channel structure, and the unified entanglement meta theorem to build a communication system organised around the DAANSphere's 153,600 node addressing — the "Azimuthal" system that gives the volume its name. The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records \sum_i w_i\, S_\text{ent}(i) = Q_\text{total} = \text{conserved}; \Omega; O(n^\pi). These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.

The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.
